articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Garang Kuol scores first Australia goal as World Cup feelgood factor continues
article.description: Australia drew a line under their World Cup exploits and gave a glimpse into the future with an impressive 3-1 win over Ecuador.
article.linkText: Kuol nets 1st Australia goal in win over Ecuador

images.name: Garang Kuol celebrates Socceroos goal [1296x729]
images.credit: Photo by Damian Briggs/Speed Media/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

peers.name: Garang Kuol celebrates Socceroos goal [1296x1296]
peers.credit: Photo by Damian Briggs/Speed Media/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

peers.name: Garang Kuol celebrates Socceroos goal [1296x729]
peers.credit: Photo by Damian Briggs/Speed Media/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

peers.name: Garang Kuol celebrates Socceroos goal [1296x864]
peers.credit: Photo by Damian Briggs/Speed Media/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

peers.name: Garang Kuol celebrates Socceroos goal [1296x518]
peers.credit: Photo by Damian Briggs/Speed Media/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

article.story:
Australia drew a line under their World Cup exploits and gave a glimpse of the potentially bright future that awaits them with an impressive 3-1 win over Ecuador.

Socceroos boss Graham Arnold has spoken of wanting to use last year's round-of-16 exit as a springboard towards the 2026 tournament.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

And the crowd of 20, 268 at CommBank Stadium in Sydney got an insight into what lies ahead over the next three-and-a-half years.

Australia's opening two goals on Friday came from experienced campaigners in Jackson Irvine and Awer Mabil in a typically spirited and workmanlike showing.

But the real moment of excitement came late in the second half when 19-year-old debutant Alex Robertson kickstarted a move which led to fellow teenager Garang Kuol grabbing his first international goal.

Uncapped Melbourne City midfielder Aiden O'Neill was the only player in Arnold's starting XI who didn't go to the World Cup and it allowed Australia to quickly get into their rhythm.

One nice break from Mitchell Duke failed to yield a goal but the forward was causing all sorts of issues for the Ecuadorians, and it wasn't long before he set up Irvine for Australia's opener.

Duke slid low to turn a Craig Goodwin free kick back across the six-yard box in the 12th minute and Irvine was given the simplest job to tap in.

Replays showed Duke was offside but with no VAR in use, Australia had the lead.

Ecuador hit back quickly with defender Felix Torres rising above Irvine to head a 23rd-minute cross from Jeremy Sarmiento past Mat Ryan to draw the South Americans level.

Sarmiento's night ended when he limped off with an injury on the half hour mark and Australia were able to re-establish an advantage soon after when they caught the visitors out at the back.

Defender Piero Hincape had a ball snatched off him by a pressing Irvine and the midfielder was able to tee Mabil up in the box.

The winger powered the ball into the back of the net and Australia looked promising.

Duke was at his bustling best and the forward could consider himself unlucky to have not scored on his return to Sydney's west.

The forward was taken off soon after the hour mark with hardworking midfielder Keanu Baccus at the expense Wanderers forward Brandon Borrello and Robertson.

Robertson, who has yet to start a senior game for Manchester City, looked assured on his maiden Socceroos outing and it was his intricate build-up play that began a move for Kuol's goal.

Robertson charged down the left exchanging passes on the way before finding Aziz Behich, whose cross was tapped in by Kuol.

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Australia 1, Ecuador 0. Jackson Irvine (Australia) right footed shot from the left side of the six yard box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Mitchell Duke   following a set piece situation.
Aziz Behich (Australia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Australia 1, Ecuador 1. Félix Torres (Ecuador) header from very close range to the top right corner. Assisted by Jeremy Sarmiento with a cross   following a set piece situation.
Substitution, Ecuador. Alexander Alvarado replaces Jeremy Sarmiento because of an injury.
Goal!  Australia 2, Ecuador 1. Awer Mabil (Australia) right footed shot from the right side of the box to the top right corner.
Substitution, Australia. Brandon Borrello replaces Mitchell Duke.
Substitution, Australia. Alex Robertson replaces Keanu Baccus.
Substitution, Ecuador. Xavier Arreaga replaces Ángelo Preciado.
Substitution, Ecuador. Kevin Rodríguez replaces Michael Estrada.
Substitution, Ecuador. Junior Sornoza replaces Ángel Mena.
Substitution, Australia. Marco Tilio replaces Craig Goodwin.
Substitution, Australia. Garang Kuol replaces Awer Mabil.
Goal!  Australia 3, Ecuador 1. Garang Kuol (Australia) left footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Aziz Behich.
Substitution, Australia. Connor Metcalfe replaces Jackson Irvine.
